title: oenothera cinerea subsp. parksii
intro: Oenothera cinerea subsp. parksii, also known as Parks' evening primrose, is a plant species within the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). It is primarily valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid with anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving symptoms associated with menopause, as well as supporting skin health and cardiovascular well-being. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal agent require further research and validation.
